name: The Curator's Exit
description:
We've received a communication from Interpol, containing a PDF. We are unsure of the contents, but believe it contains information on one of the thieves from the Louvre Heist. Your job is to find out as much as you can about this individual and send the information back to the authorities.

What is the thief's name?

**FLAG FORMAT:** `CIT{First_Last}`

**SHA1:** `c3c8f91ed60e902be482dc26b61a9bc0fa443f26`
author: elemental

name: The Curator's Exit - Part 2
description: Check the archives
author: elemental

name: Welcome to CTF@CIT 2026!
description:
Like the challenge name says, welcome to CTF@CIT 2026!

This is our third annual CTF and we're looking forward to it being better than the last. To returning players, welcome back! To new faces, welcome! Make sure you get familiar with the [rules](https://ctf.cyber-cit.club/rules) and make sure to check out our [sponsors](https://ctf.cyber-cit.club/sponsors) who play a huge part in making this happen every year!

WE WILL HAVE NEW CHALLENGES THROUGHOUT THE WEEKEND!

The flag format this year is `CIT{flag}`

If the flag format changes for a challenge, the challenge description will explicitly mention what the flag format will be. If you have any questions on this or anything else throughout the CTF, please reach out to CIT ModMail on discord!

 glhf :)

`CIT{w3lc0m3_to_C1T_2026}`

name: Rules
description:
Please make sure you read and understand all of the rules for the CTF!

1. We reserve the right to modify any existing rules or create new rules during the competition as we see fit.
2. Teams of **up to 5 members** are allowed due to prizing structure (**SCHOOLS CAN HAVE MULTIPLE TEAMS!)**.
3. Do **NOT** attack CTF@CIT infrastructure in any way. Our infrastructure is strictly out of scope.
4. Do not share accounts to bypass team limits, each team member should have their own account.
5. Prizes are limited to participants with a **VALID .edu email (or your region's equivalent)**.
6. Participants are **NOT** allowed to share flags or solutions with each other.
7. Please save any publishing of writeups until **AFTER** the CTF concludes.
8. **DO** have fun!

Breaking any of these rules may lead to disqualification from the event. If you have any questions regarding the rules or anything else during the event, please reach out to CIT ModMail on Discord!

`CIT{i_read_and_understand_and_will_follow_the_rules!}`

name: The click that may have fixed
description:
So one of the CTF@CIT 2026 players tried to download more RAM and it seems they may have gotten pwned. They said they got to one website, but the captcha required them to run some PowerShell..

Can you identify what time that website was last visited?

**FLAG FORMAT:** `CIT{YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ}`

**SHA1:** `aa50aa4516d0bc7b0aa23139f95d38edd916164a`
author: boom

name: Ping Pong
description:
What website does the PowerShell script they executed ping?

**FLAG FORMAT:** `CIT{website}`
author: boom

name: Start Me Up
description:
Are we dealing with TrickBot here or something? What's with the persistence?!

Use the challenge.zip from "The click that may have fixed" to solve this challenge :)
author: boom

name: You gotta run, run, run, run, run
description:
Waiter, waiter! More persistence mechanisms please!!

Yet another persistence mechanism seems to have been setup. It's funny because I remember the user saying everytime they logged into their system, something just felt odd when they'd see some sort of black box flash on their screen. There must be a name associated with what this is..

**SHA1:** `cc0060d01e8dc3fe69a8ca888c203bc9e57959e1`

**FLAG FORMAT:** `CIT{XXXXXXXXXXXX}`
